ddls:
CREATE TABLE "message_types" (
  "message_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message type */,
  "message_type_code" TEXT /* Code representing the message type (e.g., 0x81) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the message type */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key reference to the standards table */,
  "section" TEXT /* Specific section in the standard that the message type pertains to */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the message type (e.g., Mbps) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_types_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES standards ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_rates" (
  "data_rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for data rate record */,
  "message_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key reference to message types */,
  "rate_type" TEXT /* Type of rate (e.g., Actual, Minimum, Attainable) */,
  "information" TEXT /* Detailed information about the rate */,
  "mandatory_optional" TEXT /* Indicates if the information is mandatory or optional */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data rate */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data rate information was last updated */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data rate information (e.g., internal, external) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_rates_message_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_type_id") REFERENCES message_types ("message_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the standard (e.g., ITU-T G.997) */,
  "reference" TEXT /* Reference details for the standard */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Name of the organization that published the standard */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the standard was published */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the standard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_collection_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for data collection method */,
  "method_description" TEXT /* Description of the method used (e.g., Automated, Manual) */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the method */,
  "reliability" TEXT /* Reliability of the method (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the method description */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "networks" (
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network configuration */,
  "network_name" TEXT /* Name of the network */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network (e.g., DSL, Fiber) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the network */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the network (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_recordings" (
  "recording_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data recording */,
  "data_rate_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to data rates */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to networks */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to data collection methods */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the recording was made */,
  "value" REAL /* Recorded data rate value */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location where the recording was made */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the devices table to link the recording to a specific device */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Overall quality score for the recording */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recording_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_recordings_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES data_collection_methods ("method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_recordings_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_recordings_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_recordings_data_rate_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_rate_id") REFERENCES data_rates ("data_rate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to users */,
  "recording_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to data recordings */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_recording_id FOREIGN KEY ("recording_id") REFERENCES data_recordings ("recording_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "recording_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to data recordings */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of alert (e.g., Critical, Warning) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Description of the resolution taken for the alert */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the users table to link the alert to the user who resolved it */,
  "resolved_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_recording_id FOREIGN KEY ("recording_id") REFERENCES data_recordings ("recording_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reporting" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to users who generated the report */,
  "recording_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to data recordings relevant to the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., Summary, Detailed) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the generated report file */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., Draft, Final) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_recording_id FOREIGN KEY ("recording_id") REFERENCES data_recordings ("recording_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the notification has been read */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the notification (e.g., Alert, Report) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the notification (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality metric entry */,
  "recording_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to data recordings */,
  "quality_metric" TEXT /* Type of quality metric (e.g., Latency, Jitter) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the quality metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the quality metric was recorded */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the quality metric */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the quality metric (e.g., Pass, Fail) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_recording_id FOREIGN KEY ("recording_id") REFERENCES data_recordings ("recording_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., Router, Sensor) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the device */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)
